waves.rule: Start from top airport backlog; update existing lounges with field evidence only.
waves.acceptance: No fabricated gates; every filled field has source URL and retrieved timestamp.

waves.rule: Use alliance finders as discovery and access evidence; operator/airline/airport page confirms canonical identity.
waves.acceptance: Alliance tier names become program tiers, not separate lounge brands.

waves.rule: Airline-owned pages outrank alliance and issuer pages for hours, status, and access policy.
waves.acceptance: Owned lounge status and closures match the cited airline page.

waves.rule: Operator detail and booking pages are preferred for prices, hours, amenities, and restrictions.
waves.acceptance: Price offers require explicit amount, currency, terms label, and official URL.

waves.rule: Issuer and pass pages prove access eligibility, not physical location when stronger sources exist.
waves.acceptance: Eligibility fields do not overwrite operator, airline, or airport location evidence.

sourceScaleEvidence.planningUse: baseline breadth and duplicate detection, not canonical truth by itself
issuer access evidence and Centurion/operator confirmation targets
card-network access evidence only unless physical inventory is public and stable
operator-owned hours, locations, facilities, and paid-access offers
operator-owned The Club, Clubrooms, and partner lounge confirmation

officialPriceResearchEvidence.researchUse: Use official operator detail and booking pages to match existing Plaza Premium-branded records and attach explicit paid-entry offers.
officialPriceResearchEvidence.firstBatchRule: Start with likely catalog matches missing prices; do not create new records unless the physical lounge identity is unambiguous.

officialPriceResearchEvidence.researchUse: Use The Club and Airport Dimensions official location/detail pages for price, hours, gate, and lounge-level identity reconciliation.
officialPriceResearchEvidence.firstBatchRule: Prefer existing Airport Dimensions and The Club records; generic airport-code-only discovery rows stay out of canonical publish.

officialPriceResearchEvidence.researchUse: Use official Aspire booking pages for public pre-book prices and operator-published hours where source rights allow bounded intake.
officialPriceResearchEvidence.firstBatchRule: Attach offers to matched Aspire records first; classify partner-operated records conservatively.

officialPriceResearchEvidence.researchUse: Use official Escape booking/location pages to refresh already-promoted Escape price evidence and close residual location gaps.
officialPriceResearchEvidence.firstBatchRule: Prioritize records without gate or terminal evidence, since most Escape price rows are already covered.

officialPriceResearchEvidence.researchUse: Use official No1 booking pages for UK lounge prices and terminal-level details, then resolve partner lounge naming collisions.
officialPriceResearchEvidence.firstBatchRule: Attach prices to existing UK airport records before expanding partner inventory.

d1EvidenceContract.rawSnapshotPolicy: R2 or local cache pointer plus hash in D1; raw HTML/JSON bodies stay out of git and public payloads.
d1EvidenceContract.publishRule: D1 is terminal only after db:catalog:push, db:catalog:smoke, D1 smoke SQL, and goal:coverage pass against the pushed catalog.

terminalBurndown.acceptance: Add approved records only from official/public source evidence, or store blocker evidence for each attempted lane.

terminalBurndown.target: >= 97% records with hours or explicit missing reason evidence
terminalBurndown.acceptance: Hours must be published by official airport, operator, airline, or verified source field evidence.

terminalBurndown.target: >= 45% records with exact gate or published near-gate text
terminalBurndown.acceptance: Exact gates and near-gates must come from published text; no map-proximity inference.

terminalBurndown.target: >= 25% records with explicit amount and currency where official pages publish offers
terminalBurndown.acceptance: Price offers require amount, currency, offer label, source URL, retrieved timestamp, and parser version.

currentWorkOrder.reason: Approved and non-Priority Pass count targets are met; prioritize hours, gate, and price evidence before adding weak records.

slices.acceptance: Use official airport pages only; exact or near-gate fields require published text and prices require explicit amount and currency.
Use official operator detail or booking pages; store amount, currency, label, source URL, retrieved timestamp, and parser version.
Airline-owned pages outrank alliance pages for owned lounge hours, gate/location, status, closures, and access policy.

sourceBacklog.rightsNote: Use only the approved source workbook or public official pages; do not bypass account controls.
Official public operator page.
Official public Amex page; treat partner eligibility as source evidence, not canonical operator data.
Official Aspire and official operator partner location surfaces; retain URL provenance for every imported field.
Official public Citi travel page; issuer-program mapping requires manual review before publishing records.
Official Collinson corporate surface; use as network evidence and licensing path, not a raw scrape target.
Official public DragonPass page; import only public program and location evidence.
Official public LoungeKey surface; licensed or partner access is required for complete record intake.
Official public Mastercard Travel Pass page; retain URL provenance for every imported field.
Fallback only; honor rate limits and cache results.
Open data enrichment only; comply with ODbL attribution and do not treat as canonical lounge inventory.
Official alliance finder; use for cross-checking access and location evidence before review.

d1SmokeQueries.fieldCoverage: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, SUM(has_hours) AS hours, SUM(has_gate) AS gates, SUM(has_price) AS prices FROM lounge_field_coverage;
d1SmokeQueries.openReviewQueue: SELECT COUNT(*) AS open_review_records FROM review_queue WHERE status = 'open';
d1SmokeQueries.staleReviewQueue: SELECT COUNT(*) AS stale_open_high_confidence FROM review_queue WHERE status = 'open' AND severity = 'high' AND datetime(opened_at) <= datetime('now', '-14 days');
d1SmokeQueries.provenance: SELECT COUNT(*) AS records_missing_provenance FROM lounge_records WHERE source_count = 0 OR canonical_json IS NULL OR canonical_json = '';

reviewQueueSla.terminalQuery: SELECT COUNT(*) AS stale_open_high_confidence FROM review_queue WHERE status = 'open' AND severity = 'high' AND datetime(opened_at) <= datetime('now', '-14 days');

guardrails: No licensed commercial global lounge feeds.
No login-only, captcha, private API, or robots bypass.
Airport pages enrich matched records before creating new physical lounges.
Prices require explicit amount and currency from official booking or payment pages.
Near-gate text is allowed only when published by the source.
